title: Assessing Package Test Reliability and Quality

what: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: Computes approximate "total coverage" metric combining: 1) line coverage percentage (`percent_cov`, 0–100), 2) conservative proxy breadth tests based counts passing    test files, functions without tests, skipped test files. final metric scales line coverage (proportion) fraction tested total function-space, : - `n_testfiles` = number passing test files (0, treated 1 avoid divide--zero), - `n_no_function_tests` = number functions lacking tests (`nrow(functions_no_tests_df)`), - `n_skipped_files` = number skipped test files (`length(tests_skipped)`), - `total_functions` = `n_tested + n_untested` `n_tested = max(n_testfiles, 1)`, - `total_cov` = (percent_cov / 100) * (n_tested / total_functions).

title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: percent_cov Numeric scalar. Line coverage percentage (0–100). functions_no_tests_df `data.frame` (`NULL`) functions lacking tests. tests_passing character vector (`NULL`) passing test file paths/names. tests_skipped character vector (`NULL`) skipped test file paths/names.

title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: named list : total_cov (numeric): Approximate total coverage (proportion 0–1). percent_cov_round (numeric): total_cov rounded 2 decimals (still 0–1). n_testfiles (integer): Count passing test files. n_no_function_tests (integer): Count functions lacking tests. n_skipped_files (integer): Count skipped test files.

title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: function intentionally conservative: - Passing test *files* used proxy tested functions. - Skipped test files contribute "untested breadth". - zero passing test files, `n_tested` set 1 avoid   divide--zero produce small non-zero denominator.

title: Locate active test paths for Bioconductor RUnit layout — get_biocgenerics_test_paths
text: path    <- .character(pkg_source_path)   testdir <- file.path(path, "inst/unitTests")   test_path_list <- find_biocgenerics_test_paths(test_pkg_data, testdir)   test_path <- normalizePath(     unlist(test_path_list, use.names = TRUE),     winslash = "/",     mustWork = FALSE   )

what: Collect all applicable nonstandard test paths for a package (internal) — get_nstf_test_path
title: Collect all applicable nonstandard test paths for a package (internal) — get_nstf_test_path
text: Detects returns relevant testing directories used R package, combining framework-specific filesystem-based checks. Supported layouts: testthat: tests/testthat/ testit (standard): tests/testit/ testit (nonstandard): tests/test-ci/ tests/test-cran/         present, using tests/ root base R tests: *.R files directly tests/ Bioconductor unit tests: inst/unitTests/

title: Collect all applicable nonstandard test paths for a package (internal) — get_nstf_test_path
Collect all applicable nonstandard test paths for a package (internal) — get_nstf_test_path
text: test_pkg_data list. list booleans describing detected test frameworks layouts. following fields consulted (optional, defaulting FALSE missing): has_testthat: logical; whether tests/testthat/ present. has_testit: logical; whether tests/testit/ nonstandard         tests/test-ci/ + tests/test-cran/ layout present. has_tests_base: logical; whether *.R test files exist directly tests/. has_bioc_unit_tests: logical; whether inst/unitTests/ exists (Bioconductor layout). testdir character. Path package tests/ directory. function also derive package root dirname(testdir) locate inst/unitTests/.

code: # \donttest{ r <- getOption("repos") # save current repo options old <- options(repos = r) r["CRAN"] = "http://cran.us.r-project.org" options(repos = r)  dp <- system.file("test-data", "test.package.0001_0.1.0.tar.gz",                   package = "test.assessr")  install_list <- set_up_pkg(dp) #> unpacking test.package.0001 locally #> unpacked test.package.0001 locally  package_installed <- install_list$package_installed pkg_source_path <- install_list$pkg_source_path  package_installed <- install_package_local(pkg_source_path) #> installing test.package.0001 locally #> test.package.0001 is already installed package_installed <- TRUE  if (package_installed == TRUE) {    test_package <- run_coverage(pkg_source_path) } else {   message("Package installation failed.") } #> running code coverage for test.package.0001 #> code coverage for test.package.0001 successful # restore user's repo options options(old) # }

title: Run coverage with skip detection for Bioconductor RUnit-based packages — run_covr_skip_bioc_nstf
Run coverage with skip detection for Bioconductor RUnit-based packages — run_covr_skip_bioc_nstf
text: pkg_source_path Character scalar. Path root package source. test_pkg_data list environment test metadata (e.g., fields like `bioc_run_ut_path` (path `tests/run_unitTests.R`, present), `has_BioG_test` (logical flag), etc.).

title: Run coverage with skip detection for Bioconductor RUnit-based packages — run_covr_skip_bioc_nstf
text: `covr` coverage object success; `NULL` failure (via `cleanup_and_return_null()`). covr coverage object, NULL failure.

title: Run coverage with skip detection for Bioconductor RUnit-based packages — run_covr_skip_bioc_nstf
text: function: 1. Builds source--test mapping (using existing `get_source_test_mapping_nstf()`). 2. Removes `run_unitTests.R` wrapper mapping (via `remove_run_unitTests_mapping()`). 3. Prepares development environment **** running RUnit tests:    - Detaches commonly attached BioC infrastructure packages (`IRanges`, `S4Vectors`, `BiocGenerics`)      avoid namespace/shim conflicts.    - Calls `pkgload::load_all()` load package test.    - Clears `R_TESTS` avoid R CMD check harness interference running RUnit programmatically. 4. Identifies problem/skip-able tests via RUnit-aware `check_covr_skip_nstf()` (use    `RUnit::runTestFile()` + `RUnit::getErrors()` per file). 5. Builds list test files **`inst/unitTests`** excluding skipped. 6. Exposes namespace objects + datasets dedicated environment runs coverage    `covr::environment_coverage()`. Use function **** packages follow Bioconductor RUnit layout: - tests located `inst/unitTests/`, /- `tests/run_unitTests.R` wrapper exists calls internal `.test()` / `testPackage()` runner.

title: Run coverage with skip detection for Bioconductor RUnit-based packages — run_covr_skip_bioc_nstf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ r = getOption("repos") r["CRAN"] = "http://cran.us.r-project.org" options(repos = r)  dp <- system.file("test-data",    "BiocGenerics_0.56.0.tar.gz",    package = "test.assessr")  # set up package install_list <- set_up_pkg(dp)  package_installed <- install_list$package_installed pkg_source_path <- install_list$pkg_source_path  # install package locally to ensure test works package_installed <- install_package_local(pkg_source_path) package_installed <- TRUE  test_pkg_data <- check_pkg_tests_and_snaps(pkg_source_path) test_covr_list <- run_covr_skip_bioc_nstf(pkg_source_path, test_pkg_data)  options(old) } # }

text: First package version Package features include: Run test coverage : standard testing frameworks (STF) - tests located testthat, testit, non-standard testing frameworks (NSTF) - tests located inst sub-folders /use RUnit testing package. exclude tests errors, warnings, skips give approximate coverage. report STF NSTF test coverage. inspired covrpage mpn.scorecard.
